hero_title: Connect Sanctions and Adverse Media Screens
hero_subtitle: SFTY integrates sanctions, PEP, and adverse media checks into a unified compliance workflow that consolidates provider outputs, preserves audit evidence, and streamlines case management for VASPs.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Sanctions screening remains disconnected from workflows
problem_description: Compliance teams often receive sanctions, PEP and adverse media results in isolated systems, creating gaps in risk visibility and case context. This fragmentation slows review processes and weakens audit trails across digital asset compliance operations.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects sanctions, PEP and adverse media provider outputs with case management, decision logs and evidence so VASP compliance teams gain a unified, auditable and traceable review process.
workflow_title: From sanctions screening to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY connects sanctions, PEP and adverse media results through risk rules, review workflows, cases, decisions and evidence for structured compliance management.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented alerts. One compliance layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects sanctions, PEP and adverse media results into structured workflows, cases, decisions and evidence without replacing existing screening providers.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs in integrating sanctions and adverse media results into audit-ready workflows with controlled evidence, bank due diligence and scalable enterprise deployment.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how sanctions screening integrates with compliance

ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence is preserved for screening workflows
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe provider orchestration models for multi-vendor screening data
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for sanctions screening workflows?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects sanctions, PEP and adverse media outputs into structured workflows, cases and compliance actions tailored for VASPs and digital asset businesses.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY manage sanctions and adverse media cases?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes screening alerts into case management with policy-driven review, escalation, decision logging and linkage to customer profiles and transaction risk.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready screening evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves screening results, decision logs and evidence packages to support audit readiness and regulatory review within compliance workflows.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing screening providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY operates above existing providers, normalizing output and orchestrating their results without replacing or disrupting current vendor integrations.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace sanctions or adverse media providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace sanctions or adverse media providers but turns their outputs into connected, auditable workflows and compliance records.
final_title: Connect sanctions screening into controlled workflows
final_description: See how SFTY integrates sanctions, PEP and adverse media outputs into connected cases, evidence and compliance review for VASPs.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
